from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about an artist into a single fluent sentence.
    Handles a variety of predicates with simple natural‑language templates.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates.
    # The placeholder {s} is subject, {o} is object.
    templates = {
        "birthDate": "{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathDate": "{s} died on {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "origin": "{s} is from {o}",
        "nationality": "{s} is {o}",
        "genre": "{s} performs {o}",
        "instrument": "{s} plays the {o}",
        "occupation": "{s} works as {o}",
        "recordLabel": "{s} is signed to {o}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "{s} is associated with {o}",
        "background": "{s} has a background as {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "{s} started their career in {o}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "{s} ended their active years in {o}",
        "alternativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "language": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "{s} has {o} as an official language",
        "capital": "{s} has its capital at {o}",
        "country": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} is headed by a {o}",
        "populationDensity": "{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "areaTotal": "{s} covers an area of {o} km²",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} lies at an elevation of {o} meters",
        "postalCode": "{s} uses the postal code {o}",
        "meaning": "{s} means \"{o}\"",
        "longName": "{s} is formally known as \"{o}\"",
        "foundingDate": "{s} was founded on {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} residents are called {o}",
        "anthem": "{s} has the anthem {o}",
        "derivative": "{s} gave rise to {o}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "{s} fuses {o}",
        "musicSubgenre": "{s} includes the subgenre {o}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "{s} originates from {o}",
        # generic fallback
        "__default__": "{s} {p} {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to create a more natural combined sentence.
    subject_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for s, tlist in subject_map.items():
        clause_parts: list[str] = []
        for idx, t in enumerate(tlist):
            p, o = t.predicate, t.object
            tmpl = templates.get(p, templates["__default__"])
            # For the first predicate of a subject keep the subject in the clause.
            # For subsequent predicates omit the subject to avoid repetition.
            if idx == 0:
                clause = tmpl.format(s=s, p=p, o=o)
            else:
                clause = tmpl.format(s="", p=p, o=o).lstrip()
            clause_parts.append(clause)
        # Join the predicate clauses for this subject.
        if len(clause_parts) == 1:
            subject_sentence = clause_parts[0]
        else:
            subject_sentence = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
        sentences.append(subject_sentence)

    # Combine different subjects into one sentence.
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        sentence = sentences[0]
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(sentences)

    # Capitalise first letter and ensure it ends with a period.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:].rstrip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
